behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is watcher behavior over real directory trees, not a single function. MOCK-MISS: this is not pure parsing/logic; it appears at module handoff (watcher -> path resolver -> pipeline). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: recursive watch semantics are OS/filesystem dependent; mocked events can miss real non-recursive watcher failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Matrix row used: Integration vs System (real OS filesystem watch behavior is decisive). Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text requires actual watched-root/subdirectory observation while daemon is running, which depends on real watcher semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a file in a nested subdirectory, and assert one transformed output appears without directly invoking internal handlers.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local dedup state/time-window logic in one component. MOCK-MISS: behavior is exactly one-function/object policy, so Unit is sufficient. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; fake clock is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Matrix row used: Unit vs Integration (single local object boundary is decisive). Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text focuses on dedup logic for same path within window, with no required cross-module or OS condition.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedup component with configurable window and fake clock; submit same path twice within window and assert second request is rejected, then accepted after window expires.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets OS duplicate-event emission. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: synthetic single callbacks or idealized duplicates can miss real watcher burst patterns and timing. REAL-INFRA: requires real filesystem event stream from OS. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Matrix row used: Integration vs System (explicit OS event behavior is decisive). Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly says 'operating system emits multiple filesystem events'.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, perform one real file write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+rename/flush) and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is live watcher lifecycle under directory delete/recreate OS events. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: in-process stubs often do not reproduce invalidated watch handles and re-subscription edge cases. REAL-INFRA: requires real filesystem operations and OS watch state transitions. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Matrix row used: Integration vs System (real OS watcher handle behavior is decisive). Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text requires remove/recreate while daemon continues running, which is an OS-level watch invalidation/recovery scenario.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, delete that directory, recreate it at same path, then add a file and assert it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is ordering/state handoff across local modules (event intake -> queue/scheduler -> processor), not inherently OS watcher correctness. MOCK-MISS: unit-only test would miss module interaction/order propagation. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced by wiring modules with controlled file metadata/event inputs; no required subprocess/network. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS primitive beyond local module wiring for this behavior, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-involved handoff. Matrix row used: Unit vs Integration (cross-module ordering handoff is decisive). Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text constrains ordering policy by timestamps, not OS watcher anomaly handling.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake, queue, and processing modules together; feed simultaneous arrival requests with distinct creation timestamps and assert processing order matches timestamp sort.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real write-in-progress timing and filesystem visibility/locking semantics. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: mocked 'is complete' checks can pass while real partial-write races fail. REAL-INFRA: requires real concurrent writer and watcher behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Matrix row used: Integration vs System (real OS file-write race behavior is decisive). Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly depends on arrival event firing before write completion, a real timing/IO race.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon, begin a slow/streamed write to a real file in watched dir, verify processing does not start until writer closes/flushing completes, then verify exactly one successful transformed output.
